concepts.title: Biodiversity hotspots
concepts.description: Biodiversity hotspots are ecosystems with exceptionally high levels of endemism and biodiversity loss. They are usually small in area, but have a disproportionately large number of endemic species.

concepts.title: Protected Areas
concepts.description: Protected areas are designated areas for conservation of biodiversity. They can be national parks, wildlife sanctuaries, biosphere reserves, or conservation reserves.

concepts.title: Red Data Book
concepts.description: The Red Data Book is a list of endangered species compiled by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN). It helps in identifying species that require conservation efforts.

concepts.title: Ecosystem Services
concepts.description: Mnemonics: Ecosystem Services = 'Ecosystem Services Provide Essential Resources and Supports Life' (E - Ecosystem, P - Provide, E - Essential, R - Resources, S - Supports, L - Life).

concepts.title: Conservation Methods
concepts.description: Students often confuse 'in situ' and 'ex situ' conservation methods. 'In situ' conservation involves conserving species within their natural habitats, while 'ex situ' conservation involves conserving species outside their natural habitats, such as in zoos or botanical gardens.

concepts.title: Biodiversity Indices
concepts.description: Biodiversity indices include the Shannon-Weaver diversity index, Simpson's diversity index, and the Evenness index. These indices help in measuring and comparing biodiversity across different ecosystems.

concepts.title: Biodiversity Loss Causes
concepts.description: Human activities such as deforestation, habitat fragmentation, pollution, overexploitation of resources, climate change, and invasive species are major causes of biodiversity loss.

concepts.title: Sustainable Development
concepts.description: Sustainable development is a development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. It involves conserving biodiversity and natural resources.
